crime: Her two circles traded notes for the first time at midday. Each had taken her for the other's; the victim asked the room, laughing, whose she actually was. No one answered.

The victim's circle named its evening gathering aloud in that same room — a guest-house room, after supper. That evening she walked in among the closed eight, a box of kept things among them; their stock arrived in her, and each member took her for another member's near-one.

The victim, laughing, claimed her — ours, then — and seated her. At evening's end two always stayed to put the room right and pack the box — a fixed order every member had learned on her own turns. She claimed that turn with the victim in the circle's words; the displaced member ceded.
The members left. The victim knelt at the box; she took the cord from it and held it around the victim's throat from behind until breath stopped.

She put the room right, packed the box, tied the cord back on it, and returned before the bell. At lock bell one cell stood empty; the victim lay in the circle's closed room among put-away things, a cord-line bruise at her throat.
Only the gathering knew who had stayed behind. Eight members and a ninth: each of the eight had taken her for another's, and every attribution failed. The sole aloud claim was the dead girl's joke.

She had never sat with them before that night, yet she answered signs predating the evening and used terms no afternoon of coaching could supply. The box was packed to no member's order. It could only be done with her magic.
All eight members witnessed her claim the turn; she was seen returning alone before the bell. The victim never returned.
fields.epitaph: She belonged wherever she stood, and nowhere the moment she stepped away.
execution: A single closed door stood in a heavy frame on the execution stand. The door was keyed to the circle's own word.
She was set alone before it. No word came.
The door never opened. The frame closed on her.

magic.name: Assimilate
magic.text: Among any gathered group, the group's shared stock arrives in her — the in-jokes, references, and private signs — and she reads as native for as long as she stands among them.
